Title: Arabidopsis uses two gluconeogenic gateways for organic acids to fuel seedling establishment

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fuels post-germinative growth and seedling establishment
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: organic acid to PEP gateway from C4 dicarboxylic acids derived mainly from lipid breakdown
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control/gateway step for gluconeogenesis from dicarboxylic acids during seedling establishment
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts oxaloacetic acid to phosphoenolpyruvate for entry of C4 dicarboxylic acids into gluconeogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: strongly induced after germination; highest around 2 days after imbibition; detected first in radicle and micropylar aleurone, then embryo cotyledons and hypocotyl; also persists across attached aleurone
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: strongly induced after germination; highest around 2 days after imbibition in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native PCK1 promoter in PCK1::uidA translational fusion; vein-specific enhancer trap/UAS system for RNAi knockdown
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: post-germinative growth; dark versus light growth; delayed access to light; reduced light intensity; short-day conditions; exogenous glucose rescue; ABA represses embryo PCK1 transcription as discussed from prior work
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout mutant analysis; double-mutant analysis; genomic promoter-GUS expression; immunoblotting; metabolite profiling; radiolabel feeding with [2-14C]acetate and [U-14C]alanine; genetic complementation context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes soluble sugar accumulation and efficient use of lipid-derived carbon during seedling establishment; loss reduces sugar accumulation and seedling establishment, especially under suboptimal light
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PCK1 is induced after germination and accumulates strongly in early seedlings. 2. pck1 mutants show ~70% lower soluble sugar in 2-day-old seedlings and impaired establishment under suboptimal light conditions. 3. [2-14C]acetate labeling shows PCK1 is the predominant gateway converting lipid-derived C4 dicarboxylic acids into gluconeogenic sugar. 4. In the ppdk-pck1 double mutant, loss of PCK1 together with PPDK causes severe growth defects, strongly reduced sugar accumulation, and sugar-starvation responses.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for improving seedling vigor and reserve-use efficiency, especially under poor light or delayed emergence conditions; likely best deployed with complementary gluconeogenic genes rather than alone
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss causes delayed rosette growth and flowering in establishing seedlings; severe early growth defect when combined with ppdk
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix GeneChip Arabidopsis ATH1 Genome Array; three microarray experiments per genotype; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper concludes PCK is more important for lipid-derived substrates than pyruvate-derived amino-acid substrates; chloroplastic PPDK does not compensate for PCK1 loss in this context.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fuels post-germinative growth and seedling establishment
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: organic acid to PEP gateway from pyruvate generated mainly from amino-acid/protein breakdown
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key alternative gateway step for gluconeogenesis from pyruvate during seedling establishment
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts pyruvate to phosphoenolpyruvate via ATP and phosphate-dependent dikinase activity, enabling pyruvate entry into gluconeogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PPDK enzyme; one Arabidopsis gene produces cytosolic and chloroplastic isoforms by alternate promoter usage
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: cytosolic PPDK is induced after germination, first in radicle and micropylar aleurone then embryo cotyledons and hypocotyl; protein peaks around 2 days after imbibition and remains detectable later
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: strong post-germinative induction in seedlings; cytosolic isoform coordinately accumulates with PCK1
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native cytosolic and chloroplastic PPDK promoters in translational PPDK::uidA fusions; vein-specific enhancer trap/UAS system for RNAi knockdown
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PDRP (PPDK-regulatory protein) discussed as reversible inactivator in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: post-germinative growth; dark versus light growth; delayed access to light; reduced light intensity; short-day conditions; exogenous glucose rescue; ABA preliminarily does not repress embryo cytosolic PPDK-GUS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout mutant analysis; ppdk-pck1 double-mutant analysis; genomic complementation with cytosolic versus chloroplastic PPDK; promoter-GUS expression; immunoblotting; metabolite profiling; radiolabel feeding with [U-14C]alanine and [2-14C]acetate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes soluble sugar accumulation and efficient conversion of pyruvate/amino-acid-derived carbon into sugar during seedling establishment; loss elevates alanine, increases CO2 loss from alanine, and reduces establishment under suboptimal conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cytosolic PPDK is induced after germination and accumulates coordinately with PCK1. 2. ppdk single mutants show ~30% lower soluble sugar and reduced seedling establishment under suboptimal light environments, despite modest phenotype under optimal conditions. 3. [U-14C]alanine labeling demonstrates that PPDK specifically supports gluconeogenesis from pyruvate derived from amino-acid breakdown. 4. Cytosolic, but not chloroplastic, PPDK partially complements the ppdk-pck1 double-mutant defect, identifying the cytosolic isoform as the relevant gluconeogenic enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate to improve seedling vigor and carbon recovery from seed protein reserves under suboptimal establishment environments; cytosolic isoform should be targeted specifically
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: gluconeogenic use has higher ATP cost than the PCK route; loss causes reduced survival under poor establishment conditions and severe defects when combined with pck1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix GeneChip Arabidopsis ATH1 Genome Array; three microarray experiments per genotype; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study identifies PPDK as a eukaryotic gluconeogenic enzyme in Arabidopsis; evidence indicates the chloroplastic isoform is not responsible for the seedling gluconeogenic role.
